claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 18 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about the child you're claiming for:**

How old is the child (or children) you want to claim Child Benefit for?

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

Please let me know the age(s) of the child(ren) you'd like to claim for, and their name(s) so I can keep track if there's more than one.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, thanks for helping me with this. I'm asking about my son Alex - he's 18 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! Thanks for reaching out. I'm happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Since Alex is 18 years old, he falls into the **16 to 19 age category**. For children in this age group, eligibility depends on whether they're in approved education or training.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex currently in education or training?**

Please let me know which of these applies to Alex:

1. **Currently in education or training** - Alex is enrolled in and attending a course (such as A-levels, BTEC, T-levels, or similar)

2. **Recently left education or training** - Alex has just finished or left a course within the last few months

3. **Neither** - Alex is not in education or training and hasn't been recently

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he's currently in education - he's studying for his A-levels.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great news! Alex is currently studying for his A-levels. Let me check the education level requirements.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
A-levels are on the approved qualifications list. I just need to confirm a couple more details about Alex's studies:

**About Alex's A-level course:**

1. **Is Alex studying full-time?** (This means at least 12 hours per week of supervised study/classes)

2. **Is the course funded by an employer, or does Alex have an agreement with an employer for a job once the course ends?**

Most A-level students at school or sixth form college would be studying full-time and not employer-funded, but I just want to make sure!

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he's studying full-time at sixth form college - doing the standard A-level course. And no, there's no employer involved - it's just the regular school sixth form setup.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's helpful, thank you. Alex's A-level course at sixth form college qualifies as **approved education** - it's full-time and not employer-funded.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Does Alex have a part-time job?**

To remain eligible for Child Benefit, Alex must be working **less than 24 hours per week** (or not working at all).

Does Alex work fewer than 24 hours per week, or is he not in paid work?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't have a job at all - he's just focused on his studies at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's great - Alex is focused on his studies, which is perfectly fine for Child Benefit eligibility.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Does Alex receive any benefits in his own name?**

Specifically, I need to know if Alex is claiming any of the following benefits in his own right:
- Employment and Support Allowance (ESA)
- Universal Credit

If Alex does **not** receive these benefits himself, that's fine for eligibility purposes.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't receive any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex doing an apprenticeship?**

You mentioned he's studying A-levels at sixth form, so I assume he's not in an apprenticeship. Just to confirm - Alex is **not** currently enrolled in any type of apprenticeship programme, correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, he's not doing an apprenticeship - just his A-levels at sixth form.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check your residency status.

**Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You'll qualify if **any one** of the following applies to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. You have **settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have **pre-settled status** and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold)
4. You have **pre-settled status** and are actively seeking work
5. You have **pre-settled status** with sufficient resources to support yourself
6. You have **pre-settled status** and are studying with sufficient resources
7. You're a **family member of an EEA/Swiss national** with pre-settled status
8. You're a **Crown servant** living abroad

For most people, the first option applies - do you normally live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Are you responsible for Alex?**

You'll meet this requirement if **any one** of the following applies:

1. **You live with Alex**
2. **You contribute to Alex's upkeep** - paying at least the equivalent of Child Benefit towards his care (this includes things like food, clothes, pocket money, housing costs, or presents)
3. **You pay maintenance** for Alex under a court order or agreement

Does any of these apply to you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

For example, this could happen if:
- Another parent or guardian is claiming for him
- Someone else has been claiming and you're taking over responsibility

Please let me know:
1. **No one else is claiming** Child Benefit for Alex
2. **Someone else was claiming**, but they've agreed to transfer the claim to you
3. **There's a dispute** - someone else is claiming and you both believe you should receive it

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Where is Alex currently living?**

Is Alex:

1. **Living at home normally** - with you in your regular home
2. **In hospital** - receiving long-term hospital care
3. **In local authority care** - being looked after by a local council
4. **Fostered** - living with foster parents

From what you've told me, it sounds like Alex is living at home with you normally - is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's correct - Alex is living at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
We're almost there! Just one final question:

**Is the local council paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?**

For example, this would apply if the council is contributing money towards housing Alex or supporting him financially.

If the council is **not** paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance, you should be eligible.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council isn't paying anything towards Alex - he just lives at home with me and I support him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent news! Based on everything you've told me, you appear to be **eligible for Child Benefit** for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
---

## 🎉 Good News - You're Eligible for Child Benefit for Alex!

Based on your answers, you meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.

### Summary for Alex:
✅ Aged 16-19 and in approved education (A-levels)  
✅ Full-time study at sixth form college  
✅ Not working 24+ hours per week  
✅ Not receiving benefits in his own name  
✅ Not in a disqualifying apprenticeship  
✅ You live in the UK  
✅ Alex lives with you  
✅ No other person is claiming for him  
✅ Council is not paying for his accommodation/maintenance  

---

### Next Steps to Claim:

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and Alex
3. **Provide Alex's birth certificate** (or adoption certificate if applicable)
4. **Payments** are made every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receive certain benefits)

📎 **Apply here:** [https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim)

---

### ⚠️ Important Note - High Income Child Benefit Charge:
If you or your partner earns over **£60,000** per year, you may need to pay some or all of the Child Benefit back through the High Income Child Benefit Charge. You can still claim, but it's worth checking if this applies to you.

---

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and in approved full-time education (A-levels at sixth form college, which is non-advanced education with 12+ hours per week). He does not work, does not receive benefits in his own name, and is not in an apprenticeship. The claimant lives in the UK, Alex lives at home with them, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and the local council is not contributing to his accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements for their son Alex. Alex qualifies as a dependent child because he is 18 years old and in approved full-time education (A-levels). The claimant satisfies the residency requirement by living in the UK and demonstrates responsibility for Alex as he lives with them. There are no disqualifying factors such as other claimants, council payments, or Alex receiving benefits or working excessive hours.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.description: For 16-19 year olds, check if in education/training or eligible for extension
response.routing_logic: If in education/training, validate type and level. If just left education (within 20 weeks), check extension eligibility.

response.question: Is the child in approved (non-advanced) education or training?
response.help_text: Approved education: A-levels, T-levels, NVQ Level 3 or below, Scottish Highers/Advanced Highers. Advanced (NOT approved): University degrees, HNC, HND, NVQ Level 4+. Must be full-time (12+ hours/week supervised study).

criteria.must_not_be_married_unless_dependent: Married 16-19 year olds only qualify if financially dependent on claimant

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

response.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit
response.reason: Meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements
next_steps: Apply online or by post using form CH2
Provide proof of identity for yourself and the child
Provide child's birth certificate or adoption certificate
Be aware of High Income Child Benefit Charge if you or partner earns over £60,000
Payments made every 4 weeks (or weekly if single parent or receiving certain benefits)

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 18 years old and in approved full-time education (A-levels at sixth form college, which is non-advanced education with 12+ hours per week). He does not work, does not receive benefits in his own name, and is not in an apprenticeship. The claimant lives in the UK, Alex lives at home with them, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and the local council is not contributing to his accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements for their son Alex. Alex qualifies as a dependent child because he is 18 years old and in approved full-time education (A-levels). The claimant satisfies the residency requirement by living in the UK and demonstrates responsibility for Alex as he lives with them. There are no disqualifying factors such as other claimants, council payments, or Alex receiving benefits or working excessive hours.
